title: Orchestrator: gate system
analysis: The PipelineOrchestrator in orchestrator.py manages a DAG of 9 stages (INGEST, ANALYZE, CLASSIFY, NARRATE, SCRIPT, EDL, SOURCE_ASSETS, RENDER, UPLOAD) executed in topological order via run(). The DB layer (db.py, task 38) already has pipeline_gates CRUD: init_default_gates(), get_gate(), update_gate(), get_all_gates(). Gates have columns: stage, mode (default 'auto'), status (default 'idle'), decided_at, decided_by, notes, timeout_hours. DB stage names are lowercase ('ingest', 'source') while orchestrator uses uppercase ('INGEST', 'SOURCE_ASSETS'). A mapping is needed: stage_name.lower() works for most, but 'SOURCE_ASSETS' must map to 'source'. The _emit_event method already exists on PipelineOrchestrator for emitting events to pipeline_events table. The human_review_fn mechanism is used at NARRATE stage only — passed to _run_narrate via functools.partial. For backwards compatibility, when gate mode is 'auto' and human_review_fn is provided, the callback should still be invoked (CLI usage). The gate system replaces this with a more general mechanism: 'pause' mode at NARRATE causes the pipeline to wait for external approval (web console writes to pipeline_gates). Implementation approach: (1) Add _gate_stage_name() static method for name mapping, (2) Add _check_gate() method with auto/notify/pause modes, (3) In run(), init gates and reset statuses before stage loop, (4) Before each stage execution, call _check_gate() and handle 'skipped' result, (5) For NARRATE backwards compat: if human_review_fn is set AND gate mode is 'auto', _check_gate passes through and _run_narrate uses human_review_fn as before — no change to _run_narrate needed.

steps.description: Write test class TestGateStageNameMapping with tests for _gate_stage_name(): maps 'INGEST'->'ingest', 'ANALYZE'->'analyze', 'SOURCE_ASSETS'->'source', all 9 stages map correctly. Verify it's a static method callable without instance.

Implement _gate_stage_name(stage_name) as a static method on PipelineOrchestrator. Uses a dict mapping for special cases (SOURCE_ASSETS->source) and .lower() as fallback. Also add _STAGE_TO_GATE class-level dict for the mapping.

Write test class TestCheckGateAuto with tests for _check_gate() auto mode: (1) gate fetched from DB by mapped name, (2) gate status updated to 'approved' with decided_by='system', (3) 'gate_passed' event emitted with stage info, (4) returns 'approved'. Use real in-memory CatalogDB (catalog_db fixture) with init_default_gates().

Implement _check_gate(self, stage_name) method on PipelineOrchestrator. For 'auto' mode: update gate status='approved', decided_by='system', decided_at=now; emit 'gate_passed' event; return 'approved'.
Write test class TestCheckGateNotify: (1) gate mode set to 'notify', (2) _check_gate emits 'gate_passed' event, (3) gate updated to approved with decided_by='system', (4) returns 'approved'.
Add 'notify' mode handling to _check_gate(): emit 'gate_passed' event, update gate to approved, return 'approved'. Notify behaves like auto but the event can trigger external notification systems.

Write test class TestCheckGatePause: (1) gate mode='pause', _check_gate sets status='waiting' and emits 'gate_waiting', (2) simulate external approval by having a side-effect on db.get_gate that returns 'approved' on second call, (3) verify _check_gate returns 'approved' and emits 'gate_approved'. Also test skipped status: side-effect returns 'skipped', verify 'gate_skipped' event and 'skipped' return.

Add 'pause' mode handling to _check_gate(): set gate status='waiting', emit 'gate_waiting', enter polling loop (time.sleep(2)) checking gate status via db.get_gate(). On 'approved'/'skipped': emit appropriate event and return status.

Write test TestCheckGateTimeout: gate mode='pause' with timeout_hours set to a very small value (e.g. 0.0001). Mock time.monotonic to simulate elapsed time exceeding timeout. Verify gate auto-approved with notes='auto-approved: timeout', decided_by='system', 'gate_approved' event has payload with reason='timeout', returns 'approved'.

Add timeout logic to _check_gate() pause mode: record wait_start via time.monotonic(), in each poll iteration check if timeout_hours is set and elapsed hours > timeout_hours. If so, update gate to approved with notes='auto-approved: timeout', emit 'gate_approved' with reason='timeout' payload, return 'approved'.
Write test TestCheckGateShutdown: gate mode='pause', request_shutdown() called before or during poll. Verify _check_gate returns 'skipped' without waiting. Use _reset_shutdown in setup/teardown.
Add shutdown check to _check_gate() pause mode: check shutdown_requested() in while loop condition and return 'skipped' when true.
Write test TestCheckGateNotFound: gate row not found (db.get_gate returns None). Verify _check_gate logs a warning and returns 'approved' (graceful fallback, never blocks pipeline).
Add None check at start of _check_gate(): if gate is None, log warning and return 'approved'. Wrap the entire method body in try/except for resilience (matching existing event/run tracking pattern).

Write test class TestGateInitInRun: (1) run() calls db.init_default_gates(), (2) run() resets all gate statuses to 'idle' at start, (3) if init_default_gates() raises, pipeline still proceeds (resilience). Use real catalog_db to verify gate rows.

In run(), after creating the run record (after line ~1068): call db.init_default_gates() wrapped in try/except, then iterate all stages and reset each gate status to 'idle' via db.update_gate(gate_name, status='idle').

Write test class TestGateIntegrationInRun: (1) _check_gate called before each stage, verify via mock/spy, (2) when _check_gate returns 'skipped' for a stage, that stage gets StageResult SKIPPED and its func is NOT called, (3) downstream stages still execute (gate skip doesn't propagate like error skip). Use mock stage funcs and real or mock DB.

In run() stage loop, after dependency check and before dry_run check (~line 1099): call gate_result = self._check_gate(stage_name). If gate_result == 'skipped', create StageResult(status=StageStatus.SKIPPED), log '[GATE-SKIPPED] stage_name', continue. Do NOT add to errored_stages so downstream stages still run.

Write test TestGateBackwardsCompat: (1) PipelineOrchestrator with human_review_fn=callback and NARRATE gate mode='auto' — verify _run_narrate still receives and uses the callback (existing test_narrate_calls_human_review_callback pattern still passes), (2) verify gate system doesn't interfere with _run_narrate's internal human_review_fn logic.

Verify backwards compatibility — no changes needed to _run_narrate. The gate system operates at run() loop level: _check_gate('NARRATE') passes through for 'auto' mode, then _run_narrate is called with human_review_fn already bound via functools.partial. Run full test suite to confirm no regressions.

design_decisions.decision: Stage name mapping via _gate_stage_name() static method with _STAGE_TO_GATE dict
design_decisions.rationale: DB gates use lowercase names ('ingest', 'source') while orchestrator uses uppercase ('INGEST', 'SOURCE_ASSETS'). A dedicated mapping handles the mismatch cleanly, especially SOURCE_ASSETS->source which can't be done with simple .lower(). Using a class-level dict makes it explicit and extensible.

design_decisions.decision: Gate check operates at run() loop level, not inside stage functions
design_decisions.rationale: Placing _check_gate() in the run() loop before stage.func() call means all stages get gate support uniformly without modifying individual _run_* stage functions. This preserves the separation of concerns: _run_* handles domain logic, run() handles orchestration control.

design_decisions.decision: human_review_fn preserved as-is for backwards compatibility
design_decisions.rationale: The task says 'Keep human_review_fn as a fallback: if provided AND gate mode is auto, use the callback'. Since _run_narrate already receives human_review_fn via partial binding and auto mode passes through _check_gate immediately, the existing behavior is preserved without code changes to _run_narrate.

design_decisions.decision: Graceful fallback when gate row not found — return 'approved'
design_decisions.rationale: If init_default_gates() wasn't called or a custom stage name doesn't have a gate, _check_gate() should not crash the pipeline. Logging a warning and returning 'approved' preserves the principle that gate tracking must never break the pipeline (same resilience pattern as job tracking and event emission).

design_decisions.decision: Gate init and reset wrapped in try/except for resilience
design_decisions.rationale: Following the existing pattern in run() where run tracking is wrapped in try/except — gate initialization and reset should similarly be resilient. If the DB methods fail, the pipeline proceeds with gates defaulting to 'approved'.

design_decisions.decision: Gate-skipped stages do NOT propagate to dependents (unlike error-skipped)
design_decisions.rationale: A gate skip is intentional user action (e.g., skip UPLOAD stage) — downstream stages that depend on the skipped stage may still be runnable. This is different from error-propagation where dependent stages can't run because their input is missing. The task spec says 'If result is skipped, set StageResult to SKIPPED and skip the stage' without mentioning cascading.

design_decisions.decision: Poll interval of 2 seconds for pause mode
design_decisions.rationale: Task spec explicitly specifies time.sleep(2). This is a reasonable balance between responsiveness and DB load for human-driven approval.

reuse.how: Called directly in _check_gate() and run() — no wrappers needed. get_gate returns a dict with mode, status, timeout_hours, etc.
Used in _check_gate() to emit gate_passed, gate_waiting, gate_approved, gate_skipped events. Already handles resilience (try/except).
shutdown_requested() used in _check_gate() pause mode polling loop to break on shutdown. _reset_shutdown() used in test setup/teardown.
Used in gate tests that need real DB operations (init_default_gates, get_gate, update_gate). Tests verify actual gate state changes.

reuse.what: MagicMock-based test patterns for PipelineOrchestrator.run() tests
reuse.how: Follow same pattern: replace stage funcs with MagicMock, mock db, call run(), assert results. Gate tests extend this by configuring gate states.

reuse.what: Resilience pattern (try/except with logger.warning, never propagate)
reuse.how: Same pattern for gate init, reset, and _check_gate — wrap DB calls, log warnings, never crash the pipeline.

reuse.where: Already used throughout orchestrator.py for run/job timestamps
reuse.how: Used for decided_at timestamp when updating gate status.
